doc.content: AMD Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results (May 5, 2026). Total revenue: $10.253 billion (Q1 2026). Gross margin: GAAP 53%, non-GAAP 55%. 'First quarter revenue was $10.3 billion, gross margin was 53%, operating income was $1.5 billion, net income was $1.4 billion and diluted earnings per share was $0.84. On a non-GAAP basis, gross margin was 55%, operating income was $2.5 billion, net income was $2.3 billion and diluted earnings per share was $1.37.' Data Center segment revenue was $5.775 billion, 'up 57% year-over-year, driven by strong demand for AMD EPYC processors and the continued ramp of AMD Instinct GPU shipments.' CEO Dr. Lisa Su: 'We delivered an outstanding first quarter, driven by accelerating demand for AI infrastructure, with Data Center now the primary driver of our revenue and earnings growth.' Also Su: 'Customer engagement around MI450 Series and Helios is strengthening, with leading customer forecasts exceeding our initial expectations.' And: 'We are seeing strong momentum as inferencing and agentic AI drive increasing demand for high-performance CPUs and accelerators.' CFO Jean Hu: 'First quarter results reflect strong performance across all key financial metrics, with accelerating revenue growth, earnings expansion and record quarterly free cash flow.' Q2 2026 guidance: revenue 'approximately $11.2 billion, plus or minus $300 million'; non-GAAP gross margin 'approximately 56%.' Strategic highlight: 'Meta and AMD announced plans to deploy up to 6 gigawatts of AMD Instinct GPUs, with the first 1-GW to be powered by a custom AMD Instinct MI450-based GPU.'

notes: AMD Q1 2026 official results press release. A deep extraction captures Data Center segment revenue ($5.775B, +57% YoY on EPYC/Instinct), non-GAAP gross margin (55%, Q2 guided ~56%), the ~$11.2B Q2 revenue guide, and the Meta 6-GW Instinct design win. Reward correct attribution of segment-vs-total revenue and exact margin/guidance figures; the second-source competitive read (AMD as credible NVIDIA alternative) is the signal that matters to the judge.
